items.title: Research Funding Application
items.script:
W: Have you submitted the grant application for the climate project?
M: Not yet. The deadline was extended by two weeks because the committee needs additional review time.
W: That's good news. I still need to finalize the budget section.
M: Make sure the overhead costs are itemized separately — they check that carefully.

questions.question: Why was the grant deadline extended?
options: The committee needs more review time

items.title: Graduate School Interview
items.script:
M: I just received an interview invitation from three graduate programs. I'm not sure which to prioritize.
W: Consider the faculty research focus first. If your interests align, you'll be more motivated.
M: One program offers full funding; the others only partial.
W: Financial support matters for a multi-year degree, but research fit matters more long term.

questions.question: What does the woman recommend prioritizing in graduate school choice?

items.title: International Conference Registration
items.script: Good afternoon. This is a reminder that early-bird registration for the International Linguistics Conference closes on Friday at midnight. The reduced rate of 800 yuan applies only to registered students with valid ID. Hotel blocks near the venue are nearly full. Please complete registration and accommodation booking before rates increase.

questions.question: What is the discounted registration rate for students?

items.title: Thesis Defense Scheduling
items.script:
W: Professor, I'd like to schedule my thesis defense for next month. Is that feasible?
M: It depends on whether all three committee members are available. Have you checked their calendars?
W: Two confirmed the 15th, but Professor Zhang is traveling that week.
M: Email him the 22nd as an alternative. We need all three present.

questions.question: What is preventing the defense from being scheduled on the 15th?

items.title: Urban Planning Discussion
items.script:
M: The proposal to convert the old factory district into a mixed-use neighborhood has divided the council.
W: What are the main objections?
M: Residents worry about gentrification pushing out long-term tenants.
W: The city should require that at least 30 percent of units remain affordable housing.

questions.question: What solution does the woman propose?

items.title: Technology Ethics Seminar
items.script: Today's seminar examines algorithmic bias in hiring software. Research has shown that recruitment algorithms trained on historical data can systematically disadvantage female and minority candidates. Companies are now required in some jurisdictions to audit their AI tools for discriminatory patterns before deployment.

questions.question: What are companies in some regions required to do before using AI hiring tools?

items.title: Climate Data Interpretation
items.script:
W: The latest data shows a 1.2-degree global average temperature increase since pre-industrial levels.
M: That's ahead of what most models predicted for this decade.
W: Ocean surface temperatures are the most alarming indicator. Coral bleaching is accelerating.
M: The next IPCC report will need to revise its near-term projections upward.

questions.question: Which indicator does the woman find most alarming?

items.title: Publishing Research Findings
items.script:
M: My paper was rejected by the first journal. Should I revise and resubmit or try a different outlet?
W: Read the reviewers' comments carefully. If the feedback is substantive and fair, revise first.
M: Two reviewers were positive; one had serious methodological objections.
W: Address the methodology concern with additional analysis, then resubmit. It's usually worth it.

questions.question: What does the woman advise the man to do with his rejected paper?

items.title: Cross-Cultural Communication Workshop
items.script: Effective cross-cultural communication goes beyond language fluency. Silence, for example, signals respect in many Asian contexts but discomfort in some Western interactions. Indirect communication styles, common in high-context cultures, may be misread as evasiveness. Training programs help professionals recognize and adapt to these differences.

questions.question: What may indirect communication be misread as in low-context cultures?

items.title: Academic Library Reorganization
items.script:
W: The library is moving its print journal collection to off-campus storage by December.
M: Will researchers still have access?
W: Yes, through a 48-hour retrieval request system. Digital access remains unchanged.
M: For rare materials, 48 hours is quite reasonable. Most of us use digital versions anyway.

questions.question: How long does it take to retrieve items from off-campus storage?

items.title: Startup Ecosystem Discussion
items.script:
M: The city's innovation district has attracted 200 startups in its first two years.
W: What's driving that growth?
M: Subsidized office space, proximity to the university's engineering faculty, and a government tax incentive.
W: The university connection is probably the most sustainable advantage — it provides a steady talent pipeline.

questions.question: What does the woman consider the most sustainable advantage of the innovation district?

items.title: Healthcare Workforce Shortage
items.script: A new government report projects a shortage of 200,000 nurses nationwide by 2030. Contributing factors include an ageing workforce, high burnout rates, and insufficient training program capacity. The Ministry of Health is offering tuition subsidies for nursing students who commit to working in underserved rural areas after graduation.

questions.question: What incentive is the Ministry offering to nursing students?

items.title: Museum Digitalization Project
items.script:
W: The museum completed 3D scanning of its entire ceramic collection last month.
M: Will the scans be publicly accessible?
W: Yes, a free online portal launches next spring. Educators can download models for classroom use.
M: That's a significant step. Physical access to fragile artifacts has always been restricted.

questions.question: What can educators do with the digital models?

items.title: Food Security Panel
items.script:
M: The panel today will address how vertical farming can contribute to urban food security.
W: The yield-per-square-meter figures look promising, but energy costs remain a barrier.
M: Several companies are experimenting with solar-powered vertical farms to address that.
W: If they can bring the energy cost down, this could genuinely transform city food supply chains.

questions.question: What is the main barrier to widespread vertical farming adoption?

items.title: Philosophy of Language Lecture
items.script: Language does not merely describe reality — it helps construct it. Consider how renaming a conflict a 'police action' changes public perception of its legitimacy. Politicians, advertisers, and journalists all understand that word choice shapes how people think. This insight, from speech act theory, has profound implications for media literacy.

questions.question: What does the speaker say language does beyond describing reality?
